title: Tetraplasandra lydgatei
intro: Tetraplasandra lydgatei, also known as Hawaiian hedyosmum, is a flowering plant native to the Hawaiian Islands, belonging to the family Annonaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, as well as potential antiviral and antioxidant effects. Bioactive constituents of Tetraplasandra lydgatei include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, although its use is not well-studied and more research is needed to confirm its safety and efficacy.
taxonomy: Tetraplasandra lydgatei is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. The genus Tetraplasandra is part of the order Annonales. It is native to Hawaii, specifically the islands of Kauai and Oahu. Tetraplasandra lydgatei is a shrub or small tree that grows in the understory of tropical forests. Its classification as a species within the Annonaceae family places it among the custard apples and relatives.
